claims.claim: More than 500 public comments were submitted to Alaska DNR on the Stak Energy lease before the preliminary deadline.

evidence.verbatim_quote: More than 500 public comments were received before a preliminary deadline set by the Alaska Department of Natural Resources.

evidence.outlet: Copper River Country Journal (reprinting Herz via Alaska Beacon)
evidence.verbatim_quote: More than 500 public comments were received before a preliminary deadline set by the Alaska Department of Natural Resources.

claims.suggested_phrasing: ADN (2026-07-07) and Alaska Beacon (2026-07-06) carry the same Herz text but 403 to fetch; cite Northern Journal as canonical.

claims.claim: Fewer than a dozen of the public comments endorsed the Stak project.

evidence.verbatim_quote: Fewer than a dozen comments endorsed the project.
Fewer than a dozen comments endorsed the project. The vast majority were opposed, often in harsh or strident terms

claims.claim: DNR extended the ADL 422741 public comment deadline to 4:30 p.m. Alaska Daylight Time on July 17, 2026 (extension notice ID 224224).

evidence.outlet: Alaska DNR Division of Oil and Gas (Online Public Notices)
evidence.verbatim_quote: 4:30 PM Alaska Daylight Time on July 17, 2026
the agency has since extended the deadline an extra month, to July 17

claims.claim: The original comment deadline on the Stak preliminary decision was 4:30 p.m. on June 15, 2026 (original notice ID 223817, published May 12, 2026).

evidence.outlet: Alaska DNR Division of Oil and Gas (Online Public Notices)
evidence.verbatim_quote: 4:30 PM Alaska Daylight Time on June 15, 2026

claims.claim: DNR said it extended the comment period because of significant public interest, comment volume, and requests for extension.

evidence.outlet: Alaska DNR Division of Oil and Gas (Online Public Notices)
evidence.verbatim_quote: Due to the significant public interest in the proposed lease, the Division is extending the public comment period.
due to 'the volume of comments received, public interest and requests for extension,' spokesperson Sean Clifton wrote in an email.

claims.claim: DNR's Division of Oil and Gas issued a preliminary decision to offer STAK Energy Corporation a 50-year lease.

evidence.outlet: Alaska DNR Division of Oil and Gas (Online Public Notices)
evidence.verbatim_quote: the Department of Natural Resources (DNR), Division of Oil and Gas (Division), has made a Preliminary Decision to offer a 50-year lease
Gov. Mike Dunleavy's administration considers whether to approve a 50-year lease of state land to the project's developer.

claims.claim: The proposed lease covers a 715.4-acre parcel of state land.

evidence.outlet: Alaska DNR Division of Oil and Gas (Online Public Notices)
evidence.verbatim_quote: 715.4 acre parcel
The project would occupy approximately 715.4 acres of state land

claims.claim: The parcel sits adjacent to the Dalton Highway approximately 26 miles south of Deadhorse.

evidence.outlet: Alaska DNR Division of Oil and Gas (Online Public Notices)
evidence.verbatim_quote: adjacent to the Dalton Highway approximately 26 miles south of Deadhorse
roughly one square mile just off the Dalton Highway, some 25 miles south of the North Slope oil hub of Deadhorse

claims.suggested_phrasing: Use DNR's 'approximately 26 miles south of Deadhorse' (Herz says 'some 25 miles'); say 'about 26 miles south of Deadhorse, per DNR'. Do not use 'Milepost 390' (unverified).

claims.claim: The docket is ADL 422741 with applicant STAK Energy Corporation; notice 223817 (published May 12, 2026) is the original preliminary decision notice and notice 224224 (published June 17, 2026) is the extension notice.

evidence.outlet: Alaska DNR Division of Oil and Gas (Online Public Notices)
evidence.verbatim_quote: Public Notice of Preliminary Decision: ADL 422741, Land Lease Application, STAK Energy Corporation

evidence.outlet: Alaska DNR Division of Oil and Gas (Online Public Notices)
evidence.verbatim_quote: Public Notice of Preliminary Decision: ADL 422741, Land Lease Application, STAK Energy Corporation

claims.suggested_phrasing: The DNR Division of Oil and Gas Newsroom also lists the 06/17/2026 notice.

claims.claim: Stak's John Boyle said the full project would cost more than $10 billion.

evidence.verbatim_quote: The project, which Boyle said would cost more than $10 billion, would occupy roughly one square mile just off the Dalton Highway

claims.suggested_phrasing: Attribute: 'a project its developer pegs at more than $10 billion'.

claims.claim: Stak estimated approximately $500 million for initial site evaluation, engineering, gravel road and pad construction, and initial camp and support infrastructure.

evidence.verbatim_quote: STAK has estimated approximately $500 million in site evaluation, engineering, gravel road and pad construction, and initial camp and support infrastructure.

claims.suggested_phrasing: Say 'roughly $500 million in initial work, per state lease documents as read by the Northern Alaska Environmental Center'.

claims.claim: Stak proposes 1 to 3 gigawatts of on-site natural gas-fired generation.

evidence.verbatim_quote: STAK proposes 1 to 3 gigawatts of on-site natural gas-fired generation
Its generators could produce a gigawatt or more of power

claims.claim: Northern Journal reports that a gigawatt of generation is some 30% more than the peak demand of urban Alaska's entire grid.

evidence.verbatim_quote: Its generators could produce a gigawatt or more of power, which is some 30% more than the peak demand of urban Alaska's entire grid.
Its generators could produce a gigawatt or more of power, which is some 30% more than the peak demand of urban Alaska's entire grid.

claims.suggested_phrasing: Use Herz's exact framing and attribute: 'per Northern Journal, a gigawatt or more, some 30% more than urban Alaska's entire grid peak'. Do not restate as 'Railbelt peak'.

claims.claim: Lease documents indicate full simple-cycle operation could consume roughly 350 to 500 million standard cubic feet of gas per day.

evidence.verbatim_quote: full simple-cycle operation could consume roughly 350 to 500 million standard cubic feet of gas per day.

claims.suggested_phrasing: Attribute: 'per state lease documents, as analyzed by the Northern Alaska Environmental Center'.

claims.claim: That daily gas burn annualizes to approximately 128 to 183 billion cubic feet per year.

evidence.verbatim_quote: Annualized, that equals approximately 128 to 183 billion cubic feet per year.

claims.suggested_phrasing: Same attribution as c14. Math checks: 350-500 MMcf/day x 365 = 127.8-182.5 Bcf/yr.

claims.claim: The project would require roughly 7.1 million cubic yards of gravel fill.

evidence.verbatim_quote: The project would require roughly 7.1 million cubic yards of gravel fill to build a stable working surface over permafrost.

claims.suggested_phrasing: Attribute: 'per state lease documents, as analyzed by the Northern Alaska Environmental Center'.

claims.claim: Plans include a roughly 640-acre gravel pad.

evidence.verbatim_quote: a roughly 640-acre gravel pad

claims.suggested_phrasing: Same attribution as c14.

claims.claim: Plans include a 1.8-mile gravel access road.

evidence.verbatim_quote: a 1.8-mile gravel access road

claims.suggested_phrasing: Same attribution as c14.

claims.claim: John Boyle said most comments were form letters lacking substance.

evidence.verbatim_quote: Our initial assessment is that the vast majority of the comments are form letters lacking substance

claims.suggested_phrasing: Quote exactly; it is his 'initial assessment' framing.

claims.claim: John Boyle is Stak's chief strategy officer and previously served as commissioner of Alaska's Department of Natural Resources from 2023 to 2025.

evidence.verbatim_quote: John Boyle, Stak's chief strategy officer, who previously served as commissioner of Alaska's natural resources department from 2023 to 2025

claims.claim: Opposition comments carried subject lines including 'HELL NO! To ANY DATA CENTERS' and 'NO AI'.

evidence.verbatim_quote: with subject lines like 'HELL NO! To ANY DATA CENTERS' and 'NO AI'
HELL NO! To ANY DATA CENTERS

claims.claim: One commenter urged the state to 'incorporate some AI (Alaskan intelligence) into making this decision'.

evidence.verbatim_quote: Please incorporate some AI (Alaskan intelligence) into making this decision

claims.claim: One commenter noted their message was written 'with my own brain and fingers typing'.

evidence.verbatim_quote: One commenter noted explicitly that their message had been written 'with my own brain and fingers typing,'

claims.claim: Comments arrived from Kodiak, Kotzebue, Seward, Valdez, and the North Slope village of Nuiqsut.

evidence.verbatim_quote: from Kodiak and Kotzebue to Seward, Valdez and the North Slope village of Nuiqsut

claims.claim: Formal opposition or concern also came from the Northern Alaska Environmental Center, the Alaska Public Interest Research Group, and the Alaska chapter of Backcountry Hunters and Anglers.

evidence.verbatim_quote: came in from groups including the Northern Alaska Environmental Center, the Alaska Public Interest Research Group and the Alaska chapter of a sportsman's group called Backcountry Hunters and Anglers.

claims.claim: According to Stak, average annual temperatures at the proposed site are 12 F.

evidence.verbatim_quote: Average annual temperatures at the proposed project site, according to Stak, are 12 F

claims.suggested_phrasing: Always attribute: 'according to Stak'.

claims.claim: Stak expects the development to need 10% or less of the water that typical data centers use for cooling.

evidence.verbatim_quote: the development is expected to need 10% or less of the amount of water that typical data centers use for cooling.

claims.suggested_phrasing: Say '10% or less' (not 'about 10%'), attributed to Stak.

claims.claim: After the deadline passes, DNR will assess the comments before making a final decision on the lease.

evidence.verbatim_quote: Once the deadline passes, the agency will assess the comments before it makes a final decision on the proposed land lease, Clifton said.

claims.claim: Stak said in a statement that it 'is committed to being a responsible steward of the land entrusted to us'.

evidence.verbatim_quote: is committed to being a responsible steward of the land entrusted to us

claims.suggested_phrasing: Useful for balance beside c19.

claims.claim: The Department of the Air Force is offering approximately 4,700 acres of underutilized land at JBER, Eielson AFB, and Clear Space Force Station for AI data centers.

evidence.verbatim_quote: The DAF is offering approximately 4,700 acres of underutilized land across Joint Base Elmendorf-Richardson, Eielson Air Force Base, and Clear Space Force Station.
4,700 acres located across Air Force and Space Force installations in Alaska

claims.suggested_phrasing: Program facts date from April 2026; label as background context to the June 29 deadline event.

claims.claim: Twelve parcels have been identified for potential development under the program.

evidence.verbatim_quote: Twelve parcels have been identified for potential development.
Twelve parcels have been identified for potential development

claims.claim: The Air Force announced the Alaska AI data center Enhanced Use Lease opportunity on April 10, 2026.

evidence.verbatim_quote: Under the Enhanced Use Lease model, the service leases non-excess property to partners for long-term commercial use.
April 10 announcement

claims.suggested_phrasing: Background label (pre-window).

claims.claim: The Air Force released its Request for Lease Proposal on June 5, 2026.

evidence.verbatim_quote: Having released a Request for Lease Proposal (RFLP) on June 5
The June 5 release of the RFLP has some residents concerned about potential impacts

claims.suggested_phrasing: Note: April reporting cited an earlier May 29 proposal date; the operative RFLP deadline per June reporting is June 29.

claims.claim: Developer lease proposals were due June 29, 2026.

evidence.verbatim_quote: Responses to the Air Force's Request for Lease Proposals are due by June 29.
With a June 29 deadline for interested companies to present their offers

claims.claim: The RFLP identifies five potential parcels in the Eielson AFB area.

evidence.verbatim_quote: The RFLP identifies five potential spots in the Eielson area: one near southeast North Pole, two plots across the Richardson Highway from Eielson, one smack dab in the middle of the installation and one on the installation's north side.
In the area of Eielson Air Force Base, five parcels of land are referenced in the RFLP for potential development

claims.claim: Four parcels are under consideration around Clear Space Force Station, including one that would connect to the GVEA grid from the Parks Highway.

evidence.verbatim_quote: Four parcels are considered for lease around Clear Space Force Base, including one that would be connected to the GVEA grid from the Parks Highway.

claims.suggested_phrasing: Single outlet; say 'per the Fairbanks Daily News-Miner'. Source text says 'Clear Space Force Base'; official name is Clear Space Force Station.

claims.claim: One Eielson-area parcel is less than a mile from North Pole Middle School.

evidence.verbatim_quote: One is less than a mile from North Pole Middle School.

claims.suggested_phrasing: The line appears in resident-concern context; phrase as 'one sits less than a mile from North Pole Middle School, residents noted, per the News-Miner'.

claims.claim: Resident Sarah Hollister said living near data centers is described as like having a vacuum cleaner running around the clock.

evidence.verbatim_quote: People say living close to them, it's like having a vacuum cleaner on 24 hours a day, seven days a week.

claims.suggested_phrasing: Quote exactly as above; scouts' 'vacuum cleaner on 24/7' paraphrase is NOT the verbatim.

claims.claim: Sarah Hollister raised concern about data center heat discharge affecting permafrost.

evidence.verbatim_quote: Another huge concern I have is data centers tend to let off a ton of heat, and what can that do to our permafrost?

claims.claim: Hollister pointed out that three of the proposed sites would be expected to use the GVEA grid and asked whether that would affect members' bills.

evidence.verbatim_quote: Hollister further pointed out that three of the proposed sites would be expected to use the Golden Valley Electric Association grid and asked whether that would impact members' bills.

claims.suggested_phrasing: Attribute to the resident, not to GVEA or the Air Force.

claims.claim: JBER commander Col. Lisa Mabbutt said Alaska is expected to receive up to $8 billion in Air Force investments over the next five years.

evidence.verbatim_quote: Over the next five years, the state is expected to receive up to $8 billion in investments from the Air Force, according to Air Force Col. Lisa Mabbutt, who serves as commander of JBER.

claims.suggested_phrasing: Projection, single fetched outlet: say 'up to $8 billion expected over five years, per JBER commander Col. Lisa Mabbutt'. Never drop 'up to'.

claims.claim: Under Secretary of the Air Force Matthew Lohmeier toured JBER and Eielson AFB June 22 through 24, 2026.

evidence.verbatim_quote: toured Joint Base Elmendorf-Richardson and Eielson Air Force Base from June 22 through 24

claims.suggested_phrasing: Single fetched outlet; use 'per ExecutiveGov' or as minor context only.

claims.claim: GVEA recommended that any major loads be connected to its 138-kilovolt line.

evidence.verbatim_quote: GVEA recommended that any major loads be connected to its 138-kilovolt line.

claims.suggested_phrasing: Single outlet; 'per the News-Miner'.

evidence.verbatim_quote: plans to choose proposals that 'minimize and mitigate impact and risk...to the surrounding community'

claims.suggested_phrasing: Fetched quote contains an ellipsis; paraphrase rather than quote: 'selection criteria include minimizing community impact and keeping utilities accessible and affordable, per Air & Space Forces Magazine'.

evidence.verbatim_quote: The Golden Valley Electric Association Board of Directors will weigh options on whether to invest in a fuel-based generator during an executive session on July 13.

claims.suggested_phrasing: Single fetched outlet (gvea.com 403s): say 'per the Fairbanks Daily News-Miner'. It weighs options; do not say a vote outcome is scheduled.

evidence.verbatim_quote: about $120 million for a new unit

claims.suggested_phrasing: 'roughly $120 million, per the News-Miner'.

evidence.verbatim_quote: An LM6000 can generate between 45 megawatts and 58 megawatts of power, depending on the model.

evidence.verbatim_quote: Costs to construct a new gas turbine power plant jumped by 195% from 2019 to this year, prompting a waitlist that pushed into the early 2030s.

claims.suggested_phrasing: Attribute: 'according to a Bloomberg News report' (the News-Miner's cited source).

claims.claim: Turbine order waitlists have pushed into the early 2030s, per a Bloomberg News report cited by the News-Miner.

evidence.verbatim_quote: prompting a waitlist that pushed into the early 2030s

claims.suggested_phrasing: Attribute: 'according to a Bloomberg News report'.

evidence.verbatim_quote: Demand for the units has increased due to a surge in large data centers that require a guaranteed power supply, according to a Bloomberg News report.

claims.suggested_phrasing: Keep the Bloomberg attribution in the copy.

evidence.verbatim_quote: The option to purchase an LM6000 combined-cycle fuel turbine was put on the table at GVEA's April board meeting, where it approved $50,000 to reserve an order spot.

claims.suggested_phrasing: 'per the News-Miner'.

evidence.verbatim_quote: the charge will go up by about 61 percent

claims.suggested_phrasing: Single fetched outlet quoting GVEA officials (gvea.com 403s): say 'GVEA told KUAC' or 'according to KUAC'.

evidence.verbatim_quote: cost the average residential nearly $46 more per month

claims.suggested_phrasing: Say 'nearly $46', not '$46'; attribute per KUAC.

evidence.verbatim_quote: fuel prices were about $2.50 a gallon for what we were buying for our power plants. Fast-forward to today, it's $5.10 per gallon

claims.suggested_phrasing: A GVEA official told KUAC (speaker name not captured on fetch). Note KUAC cites cold weather, lost Southcentral gas power, and war-driven diesel prices as causes; the turbine and data centers are NOT in this article. Do not attribute the fuel-charge jump to data centers.

claims.claim: House-Senate conference committee members unveiled a draft compromise AKLNG tax bill on Thursday, July 2, 2026.

evidence.outlet: Alaska Beacon via Alaska Public Media
evidence.verbatim_quote: unveiled a draft compromise bill on Thursday for the proposed Alaska LNG gas line project

claims.suggested_phrasing: Article (published Monday July 6) says 'on Thursday', i.e., July 2; if precision matters say 'unveiled last week' or 'on Thursday, July 2'.

claims.claim: The draft compromise would replace the state's property tax with a volumetric tax on gas after five years of operation or once flow reaches 500 million cubic feet per day, whichever comes first.

evidence.outlet: Alaska Beacon via Alaska Public Media
evidence.verbatim_quote: would replace the state's property tax with a volumetric tax on the gas flowing through the gas line after five years, or when the gas flow reaches 500 million cubic feet per day, whichever comes first.

claims.suggested_phrasing: It is a draft, not law: 'under the draft compromise'.

claims.claim: The draft extends the construction completion deadline for the gas line and phase one from 2032 to December 31, 2034.

evidence.outlet: Alaska Beacon via Alaska Public Media
evidence.verbatim_quote: extending a deadline for construction to be completed on the gas line and phase one from 2032 to December 31, 2034.

claims.suggested_phrasing: 'under the draft compromise'.

claims.claim: Phase one of the project is an 807-mile gas line from the North Slope to Cook Inlet.

evidence.outlet: Alaska Beacon via Alaska Public Media
evidence.verbatim_quote: an 807-mile gas line from the North Slope to Cook Inlet

claims.claim: Glenfarne owns 75% of the Alaska LNG project.

evidence.outlet: Alaska Beacon via Alaska Public Media
evidence.verbatim_quote: Glenfarne, which owns 75% of the project

evidence.outlet: Alaska Beacon via Alaska Public Media
evidence.verbatim_quote: tentatively planning for the compromise bill to go before the House and Senate for a full vote on Thursday, July 16.
They're now planning a final vote for July 16.

claims.suggested_phrasing: Keep 'tentatively planned'; the earlier July 1 vote target already slipped once.

claims.claim: Lawmakers are in a second special session called by Gov. Mike Dunleavy, scheduled to end July 19, 2026.

evidence.verbatim_quote: lawmakers are now in a second special session called by Gov. Mike Dunleavy

evidence.outlet: Alaska Beacon via Alaska Public Media
evidence.verbatim_quote: The special session is scheduled to end on July 19.

claims.suggested_phrasing: Say 'second special session'; do NOT say 'in session since June 20' (unverified; the original bill passed in an earlier June special session).

claims.claim: Dunleavy has called the corporate income tax provision a 'poison pill' and pledged to veto any bill that includes it.

evidence.outlet: Alaska Beacon via Alaska Public Media
evidence.verbatim_quote: Dunleavy has called the tax a 'poison pill' and pledged to veto any bill that includes it.

evidence.verbatim_quote: it still contains the S corp tax that the governor and the developer have said will hurt the project's ability to secure financing

claims.suggested_phrasing: Attribute the financing-harm view to the governor and Glenfarne, not as fact.

claims.claim: On the Wood River, an automated drone emerges from its box at exactly 8:30 a.m. and takes off on an automated flight path.

claims.suggested_phrasing: Keep 'likely need several thousand'; it is an estimate, not a completed count.

evidence.verbatim_quote: the department has been tracking salmon escapement since 1955

evidence.verbatim_quote: rotate through continuous shifts over 24-hours

stories_surviving: Stak Energy North Slope AI data center lease (ADL 422741): 500-plus comment flood, deadline extended to July 17
Air Force Enhanced Use Lease AI data centers at JBER, Eielson, and Clear: proposals closed June 29
GVEA weighs a roughly $120 million LM6000 turbine July 13 amid AI-data-center-driven turbine cost surge
AKLNG special session: July 2 draft tax compromise, tentative July 16 floor votes
Bristol Bay AI sockeye counting: drone-in-a-box and computer vision on the Wood River
Background: Interior Alaska wildfire UAS operations (lightning siege recon, Cecil Fire heat check)

kill_log.claim: Stak site is at Dalton Highway Milepost 390
kill_log.why_killed: No fetched page contains Milepost 390. DNR notice says 'adjacent to the Dalton Highway approximately 26 miles south of Deadhorse'; Herz says 'some 25 miles south'. Use c08 instead.

kill_log.claim: The Stak project could consume twice as much natural gas as urban Alaska's grid (Herz dek: 'The $500 million project could consume twice as much natural gas as urban Alaska's grid')
kill_log.why_killed: Returned by only one extraction pass and not reproduced on the reprint check; its '$500 million project' subject conflicts with the verified more-than-$10-billion figure. Unstable; use c14/c15 gas figures and the c13 power comparison instead.

kill_log.claim: Sarah Hollister said 'noise doesn't stop at property lines. Permafrost and sinkholes don't stop at property lines'
kill_log.why_killed: Quote not found on either fetched carrier (Alaska's News Source June 8 or News-Miner June 11). Verbatim rule for named-person quotes fails.

kill_log.claim: Air Force Alaska Enhanced Use Leases can run up to 50 years
kill_log.why_killed: Air & Space Forces Magazine applies 'up to 50 years' to five other installations announced in October, not the Alaska parcels; no fetched source states an Alaska lease term.

kill_log.claim: The AKLNG special session has run since June 20, 2026
kill_log.why_killed: No fetched page gives a June 20 start; the fetched July 6 article says lawmakers are in a SECOND special session scheduled to end July 19, and the original bill passed in an earlier June special session. Superseded by c61.
